typecheck.details: Clojure is dynamically typed — no typecheck validator configured per AGENTS.md

nonBlockingIssues.description: Test file pipeline_polish_test.clj uses clojure.string/split-lines, clojure.string/trim, clojure.string/join (lines 27, 36, 38) without requiring clojure.string in its ns form. This is the same fragile transitive loading pattern that Fix 3 was specifically addressing in production code.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding convention: Always use clojure.edn/read-string instead of clojure.core/read-string when parsing EDN from files or untrusted sources. clojure.core/read-string can execute arbitrary reader macros, making it a security risk.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature misc-pipeline-polish Fix 1 replaced clojure.core/read-string with clojure.edn/read-string in pipeline/core.clj to close a security vulnerability. AGENTS.md Coding Conventions section has no mention of this.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding convention: Always use explicit :require with aliases in ns forms rather than relying on fully-qualified namespace references (e.g., clojure.string/join) that depend on transitive loading. Transitive loading is fragile and load-order dependent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature misc-pipeline-polish Fix 3 added explicit :require for clojure.string and clojure.set in verification/core.clj. The worker's own test file (pipeline_polish_test.clj) still uses clojure.string without explicit require, showing this pattern recurrs without explicit guidance.
